message:
feat: cross-socket zombie session cleanup and prevention (gt-olb4)

After the socket isolation fix (gt-qkekp), agent sessions created on the
default tmux socket remain as zombies. This adds detection and cleanup:

- gt doctor: new cross-socket-zombies check detects agent sessions on
  the default socket when a town socket exists (fixable with --fix)
- gt down --all: sweeps cross-socket zombie sessions during shutdown
- gt up: warns about cross-socket zombies on startup with cleanup hint

files.patch:
@@ -49,6 +49,7 @@ Infrastructure checks:
 
 Cleanup checks (fixable):
   - orphan-sessions          Detect orphaned tmux sessions
+  - cross-socket-zombies     Detect agent sessions on wrong tmux socket (fixable)
   - orphan-processes         Detect orphaned Claude processes
   - session-name-format      Detect sessions with outdated naming format (fixable)
   - wisp-gc                  Detect and clean abandoned wisps (>1h)
@@ -180,6 +181,7 @@ func runDoctor(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
 	d.Register(doctor.NewMalformedSessionNameCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewOrphanSessionCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewZombieSessionCheck())
+	d.Register(doctor.NewCrossSocketZombieCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewOrphanProcessCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewWispGCCheck())
 	d.Register(doctor.NewCheckMisclassifiedWisps())
